statement.markdown: You are given a target amount of change and an unlimited number of coin denominations, and you need to find the minimum number of coins needed.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases. The first line of test case contains two numbers n and c separated by a space. The second line of test case contains coins separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: Print the minimum number of coins of each denomination used to make the change in separate lines.
statement.notes: This problem has a constraint that the number of coins of each denomination should be non-negative.

examples.explanation: Minimum number of coins to make 2 with 2 denominations (1,2) is 1(2) and 0(1)
Minimum number of coins to make 3 with 2 denominations (1,2) is 1(2) and 1(1)

hints.text: Use dynamic programming to solve the problem.

editorial.title: Coin Exchange Problem
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved by using dynamic programming. For each coin denomination, we calculate the minimum number of coins needed to make the change.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach is not feasible as it takes O(n*c) time complexity. We should consider using dynamic programming to solve the problem.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a 2D array to store the minimum number of coins needed to make each amount.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n*c), where n is the target amount and c is the number of coin denominations.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proved using mathematical induction.
editorial.dryRun: We can start the dry run by considering the base case where the target amount is 0.

functionSignature.java: int minCoins(int[] coins, int n, int c);
